WP2P4-Descriptive Analysis: summary tables and figures

Arnaud

2026-07-05

Dataset

dataset <- read_csv(
  here("output", "dataset_patients_combined.csv.gz"),
  col_types = cols(
    patient_id = col_integer(),
    age = col_double()
  )
)

dataset <- dataset %>%
  select( 
  patient_id,
  index_date,sex,
  imd,ethnicity,
  stp,region,
  has_medication,numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti,
  numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti
)
dataset$index_date <- as.Date(dataset$index_date)
head(dataset)
## # A tibble: 6 × 10
##   patient_id index_date sex     imd     ethnicity    stp   region has_medication
##        <int> <date>     <chr>   <chr>   <chr>        <chr> <chr>           <dbl>
## 1          1 2022-02-28 unknown Missing All other e… E540… London              0
## 2          2 2022-02-28 female  3       White and A… E540… North…              0
## 3          3 2022-02-28 male    Missing Other Mixed  E540… South…              0
## 4          4 2022-02-28 unknown Missing Missing      E540… North…              0
## 5          5 2022-02-28 male    2       Other South… E540… Yorks…              0
## 6          6 2022-02-28 male    Missing Missing      E540… South…              0
## # ℹ 2 more variables: numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti <dbl>,
## #   numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti <dbl>

Table 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume by region

table_0_0 <- dataset %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),

    gp_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    pf_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_nitro_volume = gp_nitro_volume + pf_nitro_volume,

    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region)

kable(
  table_0_0,
  caption = "Table 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume by region."
)
Table 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume by region.
region n_patients n_with_medication percent_with_medication gp_nitro_volume pf_nitro_volume total_nitro_volume
East 2197 0 0 0 0 0
East Midlands 624 0 0 0 0 0
London 2131 0 0 0 0 0
Missing 5628 0 0 0 0 0
North East 1747 0 0 0 0 0
North West 1924 0 0 0 0 0
South East 1609 0 0 0 0 0
South West 2168 0 0 0 0 0
West Midlands 2883 0 0 0 0 0
Yorkshire and The Humber 2400 0 0 0 0 0 

table_region_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, region) %>%
  summarise(
    gp_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    pf_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_nitro_volume = gp_nitro_volume + pf_nitro_volume,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(region != "Missing")

nitro_region_plot <- ggplot(
  table_region_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = total_nitro_volume,
    colour = region,
    group = region
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume",
    colour = "Region"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

nitro_region_plot

Figure 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume over time by region. 

# Save as PNG
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_0_region_nitrofurantoin_volume.png",
  plot = nitro_region_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 10,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

Table 0_1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by STP

table_stp <- dataset %>%
  group_by(stp) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(stp)

kable(
  table_stp,
  caption = "Table 0_1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by STP."
)

Table 1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by IMD

table_imd <- dataset %>%
  group_by(imd) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(imd)

kable(
  table_imd,
  caption = "Table 1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by IMD."
)
Table 1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by IMD.
imd n_patients n_with_medication percent_with_medication
1 (Most Deprived) 1299 0 0
2 886 0 0
3 1490 0 0
4 538 0 0
5 (Least Deprived) 1071 0 0
Missing 18027 0 0

Figure 1. Patients receiving medication over time by IMD

table_imd_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, imd) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = sum(has_medication == 0, na.rm = TRUE), # We will use 1 when using real data
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(imd != "Missing")

imd_plot <- ggplot(
  table_imd_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = n_patients,
    colour = imd,
    shape = imd,
    group = imd
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of patients",
    colour = "IMD",
    shape = "IMD"
    
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  imd_plot

Figure 1. Patients receiving medication over time by IMD. 

 # Save as PNG 
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_imd_plot.png",
  plot = imd_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

Model

Interrupted Time Series (ITS) Analysis Using GEE

# Load required packages
library(readr)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(geepack)

#--------------------------------------------------
# Load measures dataset
#--------------------------------------------------

measures <- read_csv(
  here("output", "data_patients_measures_Arnaud.csv")
)

# Inspect data
names(measures)
## [1] "measure"        "interval_start" "interval_end"   "ratio"         
## [5] "numerator"      "denominator"    "practice"       "month"
table(measures$measure)
## 
##         gp_medication_acutebronchitis_control 
##                                          4560 
##                  gp_medication_all_conditions 
##                                          4560 
##  gp_medication_conjunctivitisallergic_control 
##                                          4560 
##                        gp_medication_impetigo 
##                                          4549 
##                      gp_medication_insectbite 
##                                          4549 
##                     gp_medication_otitismedia 
##                                           584 
##                        gp_medication_shingles 
##                                          4099 
##                       gp_medication_sinusitis 
##                                          4320 
##                      gp_medication_sorethroat 
##                                          4487 
##                             gp_medication_uti 
##                                          1024 
## gp_medication_vulvovaginalcandidiasis_control 
##                                          4560 
##                  pf_medication_all_conditions 
##                                          4560 
##                        pf_medication_impetigo 
##                                          4549 
##                      pf_medication_insectbite 
##                                          4549 
##                     pf_medication_otitismedia 
##                                           584 
##                        pf_medication_shingles 
##                                          4099 
##                       pf_medication_sinusitis 
##                                          4320 
##                      pf_medication_sorethroat 
##                                          4487 
##                             pf_medication_uti 
##                                          1024
# Keep GP medication outcome only
measures <- measures %>%
  filter(measure == "gp_medication_all_conditions")

head(measures)
## # A tibble: 6 × 8
##   measure       interval_start interval_end ratio numerator denominator practice
##   <chr>         <date>         <date>       <dbl>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      406
## 2 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      976
## 3 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      737
## 4 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      710
## 5 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      987
## 6 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1      884
## # ℹ 1 more variable: month <date>
#--------------------------------------------------
# Create ITS variables
#--------------------------------------------------

measures <- measures %>%
  mutate(
    month_date = as.Date(interval_start)
  ) %>%
  group_by(practice) %>%
  arrange(month_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    T = row_number() - 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    X = if_else(month_date >= as.Date("2024-02-01"), 1, 0),
    XT = if_else(
      X == 1,
      T - min(T[X == 1]),
      0
    )
  )

head(measures)
## # A tibble: 6 × 12
##   measure       interval_start interval_end ratio numerator denominator practice
##   <chr>         <date>         <date>       <dbl>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 gp_medicatio… 2022-02-01     2022-02-28       0         0           1        9
## 2 gp_medicatio… 2022-03-01     2022-03-31       0         0           1        9
## 3 gp_medicatio… 2022-04-01     2022-04-30       0         0           1        9
## 4 gp_medicatio… 2022-05-01     2022-05-31       0         0           1        9
## 5 gp_medicatio… 2022-06-01     2022-06-30       0         0           1        9
## 6 gp_medicatio… 2022-07-01     2022-07-31       0         0           1        9
## # ℹ 5 more variables: month <date>, month_date <date>, T <dbl>, X <dbl>,
## #   XT <dbl>
#--------------------------------------------------
# Fit initial GEE model
#--------------------------------------------------

gee_model_1 <- geeglm(
  numerator ~ T + X + XT +
    offset(log(denominator)),
  id = practice,
  data = measures,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  corstr = "ar1"
)

summary(gee_model_1)
## 
## Call:
## geeglm(formula = numerator ~ T + X + XT + offset(log(denominator)), 
##     family = poisson(link = "log"), data = measures, id = practice, 
##     corstr = "ar1")
## 
##  Coefficients:
##             Estimate  Std.err Wald Pr(>|W|)    
## (Intercept)   -63878        0  Inf   <2e-16 ***
## T           -1500240        0  Inf   <2e-16 ***
## X             -31920        0  Inf   <2e-16 ***
## XT           -367080        0  Inf   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation structure = ar1 
## Estimated Scale Parameters:
## 
##             Estimate Std.err
## (Intercept) 2.22e-16       0
##   Link = identity 
## 
## Estimated Correlation Parameters:
##       Estimate Std.err
## alpha        1       0
## Number of clusters:   95  Maximum cluster size: 48
#--------------------------------------------------
# Simulate data (for illustration only)
#--------------------------------------------------

set.seed(12345)

measures <- measures %>%
  mutate(

    denominator = round(rnorm(n(), mean = 120, sd = 20)),
    denominator = pmax(denominator, 30),

    prescribing_prob =
      0.65 -
      0.0015 * T -
      0.05 * X -
      0.001 * XT,

    prescribing_prob =
      pmin(
        pmax(prescribing_prob, 0.10),
        0.95
      ),

    numerator = rbinom(
      n(),
      size = denominator,
      prob = prescribing_prob
    ),

    prescribing_rate =
      numerator / denominator
  )

#--------------------------------------------------
# Refit model
#--------------------------------------------------

gee_model_2 <- geeglm(
  numerator ~ T + X + XT +
    offset(log(denominator)),
  id = practice,
  data = measures,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  corstr = "ar1"
)

summary(gee_model_2)
## 
## Call:
## geeglm(formula = numerator ~ T + X + XT + offset(log(denominator)), 
##     family = poisson(link = "log"), data = measures, id = practice, 
##     corstr = "ar1")
## 
##  Coefficients:
##              Estimate   Std.err    Wald Pr(>|W|)    
## (Intercept) -0.431441  0.002649 26529.4  < 2e-16 ***
## T           -0.002271  0.000220   106.9  < 2e-16 ***
## X           -0.081672  0.004924   275.1  < 2e-16 ***
## XT          -0.002599  0.000346    56.3  6.1e-14 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation structure = ar1 
## Estimated Scale Parameters:
## 
##             Estimate Std.err
## (Intercept)    0.408 0.00794
##   Link = identity 
## 
## Estimated Correlation Parameters:
##       Estimate Std.err
## alpha  0.00276  0.0156
## Number of clusters:   95  Maximum cluster size: 48
#--------------------------------------------------
# Predictions
#--------------------------------------------------

measures$pred <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  type = "response"
)

#--------------------------------------------------
# Aggregate for plotting
#--------------------------------------------------

plot_data <- measures %>%
  group_by(month_date) %>%
  summarise(
    observed = mean(numerator),
    predicted = mean(pred),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#--------------------------------------------------
# Plot observed vs predicted
#--------------------------------------------------

ggplot(plot_data, aes(month_date)) +

  geom_line(
    aes(y = observed,
        colour = "Observed"),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_line(
    aes(y = predicted,
        colour = "Predicted"),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2024-02-01"),
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "red"
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      Observed = "black",
      Predicted = "blue"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "GP medication count",
    colour = "",
    title = "Interrupted Time Series using GEE",
    subtitle = "Observed and model-predicted GP prescribing"
  ) +

  theme_bw()

#--------------------------------------------------
# Counterfactual predictions
#--------------------------------------------------

measures$fitted <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  type = "response"
)

cf_data <- measures

cf_data$X <- 0
cf_data$XT <- 0

cf_data$counterfactual <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  newdata = cf_data,
  type = "response"
)

measures$counterfactual <- cf_data$counterfactual

#--------------------------------------------------
# Aggregate for counterfactual plot
#--------------------------------------------------

plot_data <- measures %>%
  group_by(month_date) %>%
  summarise(
    observed = mean(numerator),
    fitted = mean(fitted),
    counterfactual = mean(counterfactual),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#--------------------------------------------------
# Plot counterfactual
#--------------------------------------------------

ggplot(plot_data, aes(month_date)) +

  geom_line(
    aes(
      y = observed,
      colour = "Observed"
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_line(
    aes(
      y = counterfactual,
      colour = "Counterfactual"
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2024-02-01"),
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      Observed = "black",
      Counterfactual = "blue"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "GP medication count",
    colour = "",
    title = "Interrupted Time Series Using GEE",
    subtitle = "Counterfactual assumes Pharmacy First had not been introduced"
  ) +

  theme_classic()
